channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: God of War Laufey lets you play as Kratos’ dead wife and befriend a gelatinous cube
item.content:encoded:
Sony Santa Monica has revealed their next project, and the next chapter in the God of War saga, taking the rather unexpected direction of letting you play as someone other than Kratos. How about… Kratos’ dead wife?

That’s right, the rumours were true and the next God of War game lets you play as Kratos’ dead wife, Faye, aka Laufey the Just (portrayed once more by Deborah Ann Woll), who is surprisingly nimble for someone we last saw being barbecued. In fact, it opens with Kratos and Atreus laying her to rest in the woods, but as her spirit floats off, she awakens not within the Norse afterlife, but somewhere completely different. This is the Everywhen, the afterlife of the gods, where all magic comes from and where it returns to.

But she doesn’t get a greeting befitting of a god. No, the plantlife starts to attack her, and she soon comes face to face with Sekmet from the Egyptian pantheon of gods, and Begtse, the god of war from Tibetan mythology, who have her thrown into captivity. There she meets a talking gelatinous cube called Phranque (Jack Quaid), and the ribbons of a sword called Rue (Perlina Lau), which is a turn for the even more bizarre. They are the ones who reveal that this is the afterlife of the gods, as opposed to one specific mythology, and spur Faye on to rediscovering control of her powers and breaking out.

Wwith Rue’s sword in grasp – for better or worse, given the repeated warnings about this – she unleashes some fantastic fighting abilities, including a ribbon-whipping move, the ability to punch someone’s soul of their body with the Golden Hand of the Jötnar, and then stab that a bit instead, and great finishers. She’s fast and nimble, able to combine ground attacks with aerial ones.

Here’s more on how Santa Monica has created the character of Faye for her very own game.

There’s some truly titanic battling from this point in the reveal, really showing the scale and scope of what the PS5 can achieve in the battle with Begtse.

It looks excellent and we can’t wait to see more!



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/god-of-war-laufey-lets-you-play-as-kratos-dead-wife/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Until Dawn 2 revealed, and it’s coming out in 2027
item.content:encoded:
Until Dawn 2 has been revealed by Sony and developer Firesprite, coming exclusively to PS5 in 2027.

The game features the hallmarks of the original Until Dawn and the group/teen horror tales that have become fellow UK studio Supermassive’s calling card. Until Dawn 2 sees Liverpool-based Firesprite taking up the mantle after 2024’s Until Dawn remake.

Until Dawn 2 features a team of ghost hunters who will go face-to-face with real horrors for the first time. They’ve just signed a TV deal with a network willing to ship them off to an abandoned tropical island… or so they think. Brace yourself for an awful lot of people meeting grizzly ends when you make a poor choice.

Looking back on the remake, Tuff said, “Until Dawn remains a fantastic horror game with great acting and plenty of jump scares to get you out of your seat. While not essential for anyone that enjoyed the original game, this remake brings it to a new audience and is a solid update to what was already a great game.”



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/until-dawn-2-revealed-and-its-coming-out-in-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Stuntman: Hollywood has Knight Rider, Back to the Future and more
item.content:encoded:
I was busy writing up something else, but still listening to the State of Play when something happened and i let out a little yelp. The classic “womp-womp” sound of K.I.T.T. from Knight Rider played through my headphones.

The game in question is Stuntman: Hollywood in which you can “Become Hollywood’s most sought-after stuntman. Perform explosive scenes inspired by blockbuster movies, defy danger, and drift your way to stardom.”

Along with K.I.T.T.  the game also features the DeLorean from Back To The Future, and  Sonny Crockett’s iconic car in Miami Vice. The game is “Coming soon” with no release date.

It does, at least from this trailer, appear to be a single-player game, which is frustrating as I am getting big Motorstorm vibes from it.

Source: YouTube



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/stuntman-hollywood-has-knight-rider-back-to-the-future-and-more/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve takes off with an October launch date
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco and Project Aces have confirmed the release date for Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve, launching the latest entry in the hit dogfighting series on 2nd October for PS5, Xbox Series X|S and PC.

Ace Combat 8: Wings of Theve is a direct sequel to Ace Combat 7: Skies Unknown which released all the way back in 2019. This time out, you’re a fighter pilot, adrift at sea in a rescue boat until the outdated aircraft carrier, Endurance, pulls you aboard. The ageing vessel, its decks crowded with refugees, has already retreated far from the front lines.

On this ship, you’re given a new name: “Wings of Theve”. A legendary ace, a symbol of hope, a name tied to Theve, the FCU capital. But, as it turns out, that legend was a lie; it was just a story made to keep morale alive. Now, you must carry that name and take to the skies with three new comrades at your side, fighting to reclaim your lost homeland.

Ace Combat 7 earned a hefty 9/10 when it launched, where I said, “In Ace Combat 7, dogfighting games have a new Top Gun. The VR sadly turned my stomach, but the series’ trademark action is bigger and better than ever, with the spectacular weather effects introducing a level of realism and drama that will win over any wannabe Mavericks.”

I definitely can’t wait to check out some more aerial combat, and I’ll certainly try to remember more of the story this time out, with the new first-person cutscenes looking like they’ll put you right at the centre of the action.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/ace-combat-8-wings-of-theve-takes-off-with-an-october-launch-date/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword launches in September, but you can play a demo today!
item.content:encoded:
September is getting busier by the minute with today’s State of Play stream, and Onimusha: Way of the Sword is joining the fight with a 25th September launch date. But why wait when you can play a new demo today!

Here’s some blurb from the trailer a moment:

Get a taste of the exhilarating swordplay action for yourself! Experience one of the opening stages of Onimusha: Way of the Sword, an all-new entry in Capcom’s swordplay action series, in the demo available now!

Utilize parries, deflects, Issen, soul absorption, and an Oni Armament to fight your way through the Kiyomizu-dera Temple stage, and face Musashi’s formidable rival, Sasaki Ganryu.

For more on the game, check out our Onimusha: Way of the Sword preview from last August.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/onimusha-way-of-the-sword-launches-in-september-but-you-can-play-a-demo-today/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dune Awakening comes to console in September with a new single-player mode
item.content:encoded:
Last year’s hit survival game Dune: Awakening is coming to PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S on 22nd September, and it’s coming with a surprise addition.

That’s right, in addition to the online, MMO-like aspects of the survival game, Dune: Awakening is set to pick up a single player mode, so that you won’t need to have an online subscription to play, and can take things more at your own pace.

Developing…



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/dune-awakening-comes-to-console-in-september-with-a-new-single-player-mode/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dinosaur survival horror game The Lost Wild is coming to PS5
item.content:encoded:
Announced way back in 2022 for PC, Annapurna Interactive and developer Great Ape Games have joined Sony’s State of Play broadcast to announce that The Lost Wild is also coming to PS5.

Set in an overgrown Japanese research facility, The Lost Wild is a survival horror that will see you facing the dangers of dinosaurs. Lots and lots of dinosaurs. Hungry dinosaurs who think you look like a lovely, tasty snack.

You play as Saskia, a driven and experienced investigative reporter who finds themselves stranded and alone in the aftermath of a mysterious temporal event.

“The world is largely based on a “Hub and Spoke” design that allows for paced and controlled storytelling while supporting the systemic nature of the AI encounters,” say  Annapurna Interactive.

“With a sprawling, multi-pathed approach to our world design, we reward and promote thorough exploration and backtracking while enabling our AI systems to surprise and thrill the player. From overgrown maze-like structures to lush open vistas, expect nonlinear exploration underscored by a cinematic and mostly linear narrative.”

The Lost Wild will launch sometime in 2027.

Source: YouTube



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/02/dinosaur-survival-horror-game-the-lost-wild-is-coming-to-ps5/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
